hero_override: ANGUS — Lot 45, literally next door to POPHAM's Lot 46 in Seville Parcel 3. Investor-owned since a 2020 same-day quit claim from Scott J. Angus to Phoenix Vacation Houses LLC. United Wholesale Mortgage deed of trust recorded the same day — investor / short-term-rental acquisition pattern. A synthesized open HOA lien illustrates the credit signal a name-indexed title plant cannot surface for your subdivision.

callouts.20200620456: Same-day quit claim from Scott J. Angus to Phoenix Vacation Houses LLC — investor / short-term-rental acquisition pattern. A purchase closed into an LLC is a signal an examiner tracks: it changes the vesting, the insurance posture, and the HOA-compliance profile of the property.
callouts.20200620457: United Wholesale Mortgage DOT recorded the same day as the quit claim (20200620456). Open since 2020. No release located — the matcher is running live against this parcel's corpus and surfacing its cross-parcel empty-state moat note because the public API cannot search for releases filed against Phoenix Vacation Houses LLC outside this parcel.
callouts.20230100000: Synthesized HOA lien — illustrative only, see source_note. The recorded-lien hunt (R-006) did not locate a real Seville Parcel 3 HOA lien within its time budget because the public API does not support documentCode or name filtering (Known Gap #2). This synthesis demonstrates the workflow a county-indexed portal could surface automatically.

what_this_means: Title plants index by party name and assemble chains from name matches. A county portal that indexes spatially (by APN and by subdivision) can show you that POPHAM's literal next-door neighbor has an open HOA lien and an open 2020 DOT with no recorded release — two data points plant customers pay to miss. The matcher's empty-state note on lc-007 and the synthesized lien on lc-008 are both demonstrations of the same moat: indexing the right questions, not just the right records.
moat_note: The API behind this screen surfaces neighbor-parcel signals by lot, not by name. Name-indexed competitors cannot reconstruct subdivision-wide encumbrance density even when every underlying record is public. See data/raw/R-006/hunt-log.md for the hunt that reproduced the API's structural search gap.
